steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage traces Barragan's shift from historically inspired early houses to modernist designs he developed after international travels in the 1930s. The correct answer must reflect that cause-and-effect: travels abroad triggered the stylistic change.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Barragan's designs of the El Arenal Parish Church and the houses for Emiliano Robles León are considered paragons of a functionalist and modernist aesthetic.: Labels two specific projects as paragons of his career rather than showing a shift across it. Overstates and misrepresents the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — Barragan's design of the houses for Emiliano Robles León is considered more experimental than his design for the El Arenal Parish Church.: Implies a distinction between project types that the passage never draws. ✗
  • Choice C — Barragan's early work shows an initial dedication to a modernist aesthetic that he later abandoned.: Says Barragan reversed a stylistic choice, but the passage describes a forward evolution, not a reversal. ✗
  • Choice D — A notable shift in Barragan's design aesthetic reflects the influence of his time abroad.: Directly links Barragan's adoption of functionalism and modernism to his international travels, matching the passage's own cause-effect structure. ✓
That is the correct answer.
